assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.label: Mental model plus practical code reasoning
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic combines an important model with implementation consequences, so both explanation and code reasoning are required.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.

commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Explain providing and consuming context using a new example and justify the result.
Compare local versus global state using a new example and justify the result.
Compare ui versus remote state using a new example and justify the result.
Explain choosing state placement using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Providing and consuming context** → **Local versus global state** → **UI versus remote state** → **Choosing state placement**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Context API and Advanced State Management rule and the observable result.
choices.label: UI state describes current interaction. Remote state is server-owned and adds asynchronous freshness, caching, and synchronization concerns.

choices.feedback: A context provider supplies a value to descendants; consumers read the nearest provider. Context transports data but does not organize every update automatically.
choices.label: A context provider supplies a value to descendants; consumers read the nearest provider. Context transports data but does not organize every update automatically.

summary: Context API and Advanced State Management connects Providing and consuming context, Local versus global state, UI versus remote state.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Context API and Advanced State Management

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: The ideas in context api and advanced state management recur throughout later React work, reducing memorization and debugging later. It belongs to the **Context and Advanced State Management** module.
